---
title: "Pipeline example"
format: html
---

# Introduction

This file shows how to utilize the pipeline built here to generate an ERC network for some species of interest. In this case, we will be focusing on *D. melanogaster* and utilizing all annotated Dipteran genomes available on ncbi, alongside *C. felis* as an outgroup. We assume that you are using some form of Unix, but the pipeline has been tested on Ubuntu running in WSL2. It _should_ work in both Windows and on MacOS, assuming all necessary software is installed and visible to your CLI. 

### Getting data

 We first download the data, which can be made easier using the `download_and_rename_genomes.sh` script available in `scr`. Note, this script requires ncbi's `datasets-cli` command line utility. Find it [here](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/datasets/docs/v2/command-line-tools/download-and-install/), for now, before inevitable NCBI budget cuts remove access to the data. It will also require the ncbi esearch tools, installation information found [here](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK179288/)


# Initializing the software, installing all dependencies.

Before we run any analyses, we will require several tools.
1) Julia v 1.11+

Once Julia is installed, run the below script:
```{julia}
using Pkg
Pkg.add("DrWatson")
Pkg.activate("CoEvolution")
Pkg.instantiate()
```

This might run for a while, as it will compile any necessary packages for later scripts. While that is running, install the following on your system.

2) [Muscle v5+](https://github.com/rcedgar/muscle/releases)
3) [Diamond](https://github.com/bbuchfink/diamond/wiki)
4) [iqTree2](http://www.iqtree.org/#download)

Hopefully Julia is up and running at this point, so we can begin on the main steps of the analysis

# Finding orthologs for focal species.

We first identify orthologous sequences for all genes within the focal species. This is done in the following code block, most functions are defined in the `src\Finding_orthologs.jl` script. Fill out parameters in the blanks below to point to the correct files.

```{julia}
using DrWatson
@quickactivate "CoEvolution"
include(srcdir("Finding_orthologs.jl"))
include(srcdir("ERC_functions.jl"))
#Set these values for your own analysis
data_dir = datadir("Teleostei/")
focal= "G_aculeatus"
outgroup = "C_felis"

#Now run the below, and go off to grab some tea, or take a break for the day.
species = replace.(filter(endswith(".faa"),readdir(data_dir*"seqs/")),r".faa"=>"")
all_orthologs = find_orths(focal,species,data_dir)
#If the above gets interrupted - delete any .tsv files of size 0 in your `data_dir\blast` directory, and run it again. It will only run analyses for missing pairs of species.
using JLD2

jldsave(data_dir*"orthologs.jld2",orths=all_orthologs)
#And if things break later, you can always load in the all_orthologs dataframe with `all_orthologs=jldopen(data_dir*"orthologs.jld2")[orths]`

#The next step takes the longest, since parallelization is doable but not recommended (mafft can take quite a bit of memory, so expect slow downs)

build_orth_files(all_orthologs,data_dir)

#Finally, we generate a tree for each gene. Earlier versions we constrained the tree topology, which was faster, but does not allow us to do certain secondary analyses that we will want to. 
using FLoops
using ProgressMeter

isdir(data_dir*"trees/") || mkdir(data_dir*"trees/")

aligned_genes = filter(endswith(".aligned.fasta"),readdir(data_dir*"aligned/"))
finished_trees = replace.(filter(endswith(".treefile"),readdir(data_dir*"trees/")),r".treefile"=>s"")

todo = data_dir*"aligned/".*setdiff(aligned_genes, finished_trees)

p=Progress(length(todo),desc="Running IQTree2")
@floop ThreadedEx() for seq in todo
    cmd = `iqtree2 -s $seq -ntmax 4 -quiet`
    run(pipeline(cmd;stderr=devnull))
    treefile_old = seq*".treefile"
    treefile_new = replace(replace(seq,r".aligned.fasta"=>s".treefile"),r"/aligned/"=>s"/trees/")
    cmd2 = `mv $treefile_old $treefile_new`
    run(cmd2)
    try
        run(`rm $seq.mldist`)
        run(`rm $seq.model.gz`)
        run(`rm $seq.ckp.gz`)
        run(`rm $seq.iqtree`)
    catch
    end
    next!(p)
end


```
